title: Strategic Account Director
description:
**About Us****nShift** is the leading global provider of cloud delivery management solutions (SaaS), we enable the frictionless shipment and return of almost one billion shipments across 190 countries each year. We are headquartered in London and Oslo and have over 460 employees across offices in Sweden, Finland, Norway, Denmark, the United Kingdom, Poland, the Netherlands, Belgium, and Romania.
Our software is used by many of the world leading e-commerce, retail, manufacturing, and 3PL shippers due to us having over 1000 carriers integrated into our platform, nearly 3 times more than our competitors!  

If you buy goods online, there is a strong chance that nShift has powered that delivery, so come and join us as we shape the future of shipping, one frictionless journey at a time.**Strategic Account Director (Tier 1 Accounts)****Role Overview**
The Customer Director is a senior, client-facing role responsible for managing and growing strategic Tier 1 accounts across nShift’s global portfolio. Each Customer Director will own a defined portfolio of ~20–30 Tier 1 customers, acting as the trusted strategic advisor to C-level and senior stakeholders.
The primary focus of this role is customer retention and churn risk mitigation, while also identifying and delivering growth opportunities to maximise customer lifetime value. This leader will deeply understand each customer’s business, build long-term relationships, and align nShift’s solutions to deliver measurable impact, positioning us as an indispensable strategic partner.**Key Responsibilities****Strategic Account Management:**
Own and manage a portfolio of ~20–30 Tier 1 enterprise customers.
Act as the single point of contact and trusted advisor for senior executives and key decision-makers.
Build multi-level, cross-functional relationships to embed nShift across customer organisations.
Create and execute joint success plans with customers, ensuring alignment with their strategic goals.
Drive renewals with a focus on zero churn, proactively identifying and addressing risks.
**Growth and Expansion:**
Identify upsell and cross-sell opportunities to drive revenue growth within the portfolio.
Partner with Sales, Technical and Product teams to align new offerings with customer priorities.
Lead executive business reviews (MBRs/QBRs) to showcase value, drive strategic discussions, and influence budget allocation.
Map account organisations and develop strategies to expand footprint into new divisions, regions, or business units.
**Customer Advocacy and Retention:**
Serve as a champion for customers internally, influencing product roadmaps and service delivery to meet evolving needs.
Build a deep understanding of each customer’s strategy, operations, KPIs, and challenges.
Proactively monitor customer health, usage patterns, and feedback to anticipate risks and mitigate churn.
Develop executive-level engagement programs to reinforce loyalty and retention.
**Operational Excellence:**
Maintain accurate account health scoring, renewal forecasting, and revenue reporting.
Ensure consistent delivery of customer outcomes through close collaboration with Support, Services, and Operations.
Lead structured governance, including contract renewals, escalations, and strategic initiatives.
Leverage data-driven insights to demonstrate ROI and measurable value to customers.
**Key Requirements****Experience & Background:**
Proven track record (8+ years) in strategic account management, customer success, or enterprise sales with Tier 1/global accounts.
Strong experience in retention-focused roles, demonstrating the ability to reduce churn and grow revenue within large, complex customers.
Expertise in building trusted executive relationships (C-suite, VP level).
Deep understanding of SaaS, technology platforms, or enterprise services (logistics/supply chain/e-commerce expertise preferred).
**Skills & Attributes:**
Exceptional communication and negotiation skills, able to influence at all organisational levels.
Strategic thinker with the ability to align solutions to customer business outcomes.
Strong commercial acumen and ability to drive profitable account growth.
Highly organised with a structured approach to account planning and governance.
Skilled in conflict resolution, with a proactive approach to risk management.
Data-driven mindset to measure and articulate ROI and value delivery.
**Behaviours & Competencies:**
Trusted advisor with executive presence and gravitas.
Relationship builder with a consultative, solutions-oriented approach.
Strong business acumen with the ability to understand customers’ commercial priorities.
Collaborative team player who partners closely with Sales, Product, and Operations.
Resilient, proactive, and adaptable in managing complex, high-value accounts.
**Why This Role Matters**
The Strategic Account Director role is pivotal to safeguarding nShift's Tier 1 revenue stream and driving strategic growth. By building deep, trusted partnerships and ensuring measurable value delivery, this role will strengthen customer loyalty, maximise revenue opportunities, and ensure nshift remains an essential partner for our largest enterprise customers.**Please ensure you upload your CV in English**
At nShift we believe in embracing diversity in all forms and fostering an inclusive environment for everyone, which we believe is essential for our continued success. We're an equal-opportunity employer, which means that all applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, family or parental status, national origin, veteran, neurodiversity, or disability status.

title: Senior Bid Manager
description:
We’re looking for a **Senior Bid Manager** to join us at Telia Cygate.


My name is **Erling Linde** , Bid Manager & Sales Excellence, and I’m looking for a Senior Bid Manager who wants to lead complex and business-critical bids within IT, security, and digital infrastructure—while making a real impact in a collaborative and supportive environment.


In this role, you will plan, lead, and ensure the quality of bid processes—from analyzing request documentation to delivering a final proposal, supporting negotiations, and handing over to delivery. You will work closely with colleagues across sales, solution, delivery, legal, security, and finance to create competitive, clear, and sustainable business proposals.  

  



**What you’ll do**

As a Senior Bid Manager, you will:  

  



* Lead complex bid and proposal processes from start to finish
* Analyze RFP/RFT documentation and identify requirements, risks, and opportunities
* Coordinate cross-functional teams and ensure smooth collaboration and progress
* Manage information security and regulatory requirements in bids
* Contribute to go/no-go decisions, pricing, risk assessments, and overall strategy
* Write clear, engaging, and high-quality proposal content
* Support negotiations and customer clarifications
* Review and adapt contractual terms during the bid process
* Help develop our ways of working, templates, and best practices

We also see it as a strong advantage if you have an interest or experience in areas such as information security, cybersecurity, and regulatory frameworks (e.g., ISO 27001, NIS2, DORA, GDPR).


You don’t need to be a technical expert - what matters is your ability to understand requirements, identify risks, ask the right questions, and ensure clear and business-relevant responses.  

  



**What will help you thrive in this role**

We are looking for someone with experience in Bid Management, Proposal Management, or a similar role—preferably within IT, telecom, cybersecurity, managed services, cloud, networking, or the public sector.


You thrive in a collaborative environment and are comfortable navigating complexity, multiple stakeholders, and tight deadlines. You bring structure, clarity, and strong business understanding, and you communicate in a way that supports confident decision-making.


We believe you may have experience with:


* Larger RFP/RFT processes
* Public procurement
* Requirement and risk analysis
* Information security requirements and related documentation
* Working closely with sales, technical teams, legal, and delivery
* Contracts and commercial terms
* Writing clear, tailored, and customer-focused proposals
* Fluency in Swedish and English is a requirement for this position.

Even if you don’t meet every single requirement, we encourage you to apply - we value potential, mindset, and willingness to learn.


**Why Telia Cygate?**

At Telia Cygate, we’re not only experts in networks, security, and various types of cloud solutions – we’re also a team that believes work should be fun!
  

  

As part of Telia Company, we have the strength to create real impact on a large scale, contribute to societal value, and help our customers navigate the world of digitalization. And we always do it with warmth, care, and a smile. Here, it’s our ideas and diverse perspectives that make us strong, and together we ensure that the world of technology becomes a place where everyone feels good – and where you belong.  

  



Your development matters to us. We encourage continuous learning and make sure that you and your interests are in focus. We know that people are the heart of our company, which is why we value joy, creativity, and different perspectives in everything we do.


Welcome to grow together with us – where we make technology human and create real impact!  

  



**Telia Cygate** **is proud to be** **certificated** **as** **Great** **Place to** **Work!**

With us, people are at the center – here you get the chance to grow, be challenged, and thrive together with dedicated colleagues. We are building a culture where security, togetherness, and opportunities go hand in hand. Do you want to be part of the journey? Welcome to a workplace that truly lives up to its name.  

  



**What we offer**

When you join us, you'll receive a comprehensive benefits package tailored to your local market, opportunities for remote work, and more. You'll also have the chance to earn a bonus based on your performance and Telia's results.  

  



**Interested? Apply!**

We look forward to receiving your application by **2026-07-09.** Please note that we might close the position earlier if we find the right candidate, so don’t wait too long to apply!  

  



**Location:** Solna / Göteborg / Malmö  

  



If you’re selected for the next stage, you’ll be contacted by our **Talent Acquisition Partner.**  

  

Background and reference checks will be part of the process.  

  



Want to know more about this role?
  

Please reach out to Erling Linde at erling.linde@cygate.se
  

  

We kindly ask recruitment agencies and external vendors not to contact us regarding this position.  

  



**Equal opportunities**

At Telia, we recognize talent by professional skills and growth potential. We champion diversity, ensuring every individual’s needs are met, regardless of disability, gender, age, nationality, sexual orientation, or social status. Together, we’re dedicated to fostering an inclusive work environment where your uniqueness is our strength.  

  



**Welcome to Telia** **Cygate** **– Home of** **Experts**

title: Network Operations Technician Consultant
description:
Vill du kickstarta din karriär inom nätverk och arbeta med några av Sveriges mest spännande kunder? Drivs du av att lära dig snabbt, ta ansvar och vara med och bygga framtidens digitala infrastruktur? Då kan det här vara din nästa utmaning.


Jag heter Anna Lundahl och jag leder ett team av nätverksspecialister på Telia Cygate. Nu letar vi efter dig som har ett stort teknikintresse, rätt attityd och en tydlig vilja att utvecklas – tillsammans med oss.


**Om rollen**

Som Junior Nätverkstekniker hos oss blir du en viktig del i teamet som designar, implementerar och driftar nätverkslösningar för våra kunder.


Du kommer arbeta nära erfarna kollegor och få en brant utvecklingskurva – både tekniskt och i din konsultroll.


**Exempel på vad du kommer göra:**

* Arbeta med installation, konfiguration och felsökning av nätverk
* Vara med i leveranser till både nya och befintliga kunder
* Stötta i drift och utveckling av nätverks- och säkerhetslösningar
* Lära dig arbeta i komplexa miljöer – både on-prem och cloud
* Delta i kunddialoger tillsammans med seniora kollegor

Här får du chansen att bygga en riktigt stark grund inom nätverk, säkerhet och modern IT-infrastruktur.


**Vem är du?**

Vi tror att du är i början av din karriär – men viktigast är din inställning.


**Du har:**

* Relevant utbildning (t.ex. YH, högskola eller motsvarande)
* Grundläggande kunskaper inom nätverk (routing, switching)
* Intresse för teknik – du gillar att förstå hur saker faktiskt fungerar

**Och framför allt:**

* Du har driv, nyfikenhet och hunger att utvecklas
* Du tar ansvar och vågar testa
* Du gillar att jobba tillsammans med andra och lära av dem

Har du erfarenhet av Cisco, Fortinet eller liknande är det meriterande – men inget krav.


**Varför Telia Cygate?**

På Telia Cygate är vi inte bara experter på nätverk, säkerhet och olika slags moln – vi är också ett gäng som tycker att det ska vara kul att gå till jobbet! Som en del av Telia Company har vi styrkan att skapa verklig påverkan i stor skala, bidra till samhällsnytta och hjälpa våra kunder att navigera i digitaliseringens värld. Vi gör det alltid med värme, omtanke och ett leende. Här är det idéerna och våra olika perspektiv som gör oss starka, och tillsammans ser vi till att teknikens värld blir en plats där alla trivs – och där du hör hemma.


Hos oss är din utveckling viktig. Vi uppmuntrar till kontinuerlig kompetensutveckling och ser till att just du och dina intressen är i fokus. Vi vet att det är människorna som utgör vårt företag, och därför värdesätter vi glädje, kreativitet och olika perspektiv i allt vi gör.


Välkommen att växa tillsammans med oss, där vi gör teknik mänsklig och skapar verklig påverkan!


**Telia Cygate är stolta över att vara certifierade som Great Place to Work!**  

Hos oss står människor i centrum – här får du utvecklas, utmanas och trivas tillsammans med engagerade kollegor. Vi bygger en kultur där trygghet, gemenskap och möjligheter går hand i hand. Vill du vara med på resan? Välkommen till en arbetsplats som verkligen gör skäl för namnet.


**Låter det intressant?**

Vi ser fram emot din ansökan senast 2026-06-18. Rollen kan komma att stängas tidigare om vi hittar rätt kandidat – så vänta inte för länge!


Placeringsort: Norrköping


Om du går vidare i processen kommer du att kontaktas av vår Talent Acquisition Partner. Bakgrund- och referenskontroller ingår i processen.


Vill du veta mer om rollen? Kontakta gärna rekryterande chef Anna Lundahl på anna.lundahl@cygate.se


**I samband med våra rekryteringar undanber vi oss kontakter från rekryteringsföretag och andra annonsörer.**

**Lika möjligheter för alla**

På Telia värderar vi kompetens och utvecklingspotential. Vi främjar mångfald och ser till att varje individs behov tillgodoses, oavsett funktionsvariation, kön, ålder, nationalitet, sexuell läggning eller social bakgrund. Tillsammans skapar vi en inkluderande arbetsmiljö där din unikhet är vår styrka.  



  

**Välkommen till Telia Cygate – Home of Experts**

title: Teradata Architect
description:
About Cognizant
  
Cognizant is one of the world's leading professional services companies, helping organizations modernize technology, reimagine processes, and transform experiences so they stay ahead in a fast-changing world. With over 300,000 associates globally, Cognizant combines a passion for client satisfaction, technology innovation, deep industry and business process expertise, and a global, collaborative workforce that embodies the future of work. We are consistently listed among the most admired companies in the world and among the best companies to work for. Our culture is built on a foundation of transparency, inclusion, and collaboration — empowering our people to grow and thrive both professionally and personally. At Cognizant, you won't just be part of a company; you'll be part of a community that is shaping the future of industries across the globe.
  
Role Overview
  
We are looking for an experienced Teradata Architect with strong Snowflake expertise to lead and drive a large-scale data warehouse transformation within the banking sector. In this role, you will be responsible for guiding the migration from legacy Teradata environments to a modern Snowflake-based cloud data platform, ensuring a smooth, efficient, and scalable transition.
  
The role requires a combination of deep technical expertise, architectural thinking, and hands-on involvement, along with the ability to collaborate across engineering, architecture, and business teams.
  
**Location:** Stockholm
  
Key Responsibilities
  
You will take ownership of understanding the current Teradata data warehouse landscape and play a central role in driving its migration to Snowflake. This includes reviewing and validating the end-to-end migration strategy and identifying areas for improvement or optimization.
  
You will support the design and evolution of the target Snowflake architecture, including storage, compute, and security layers, ensuring alignment with business and technical requirements. A key part of the role is working closely with engineering teams to convert Teradata SQL, stored procedures, and macros into Snowflake-compatible solutions, while refactoring scripts for improved performance and scalability.
  
You will collaborate with data engineers, cloud architects, and business stakeholders to ensure alignment across the transformation initiative. In addition, you will provide technical leadership and mentorship, helping to guide teams through the migration and build best-practice capabilities.
  
The role also includes working with existing security and access control frameworks, translating current role-based access models into the Snowflake environment, and ensuring alignment with enterprise data governance and compliance standards.
  
Core Requirements
  
You bring strong experience in Teradata architecture and data warehousing, combined with advanced proficiency in SQL across both Teradata and Snowflake environments. You have hands-on experience working with Snowflake and a solid understanding of how to migrate, transform, and optimize data warehouse solutions in a cloud context.
  
You have experience leading or playing a key role in at least one full-scale data warehouse migration and are comfortable working in complex transformation environments where both legacy and modern platforms coexist.
  
Desirable Skills
  
Experience working with ETL tools is valuable, with familiarity in platforms such as IDMC considered an advantage. You bring a collaborative mindset and are used to working in cross-functional teams, contributing not only to technical delivery but also to overall solution design and continuous improvement.

title: Migration Discovery & Assessment Lead
description:
Choosing Capgemini means choosing a company where you will be empowered to shape your career in the way you’d like, where you’ll be supported and inspired by a collaborative community of colleagues around the world, and where you’ll be able to reimagine what’s possible. Join us and help the world’s leading organizations unlock the value of technology and build a more sustainable, more inclusive world.


**Make it real with Cloud Infrastructure Services (CIS) at Capgemini.**  

  

At CIS, we enable organizations to transform and thrive in a digital-first world. Our teams deliver end-to-end solutions across cloud, cybersecurity, automation, and user experience – helping clients modernize their infrastructure and unlock innovation at scale.
  

  

**Your role**
  

We are looking for an experienced **Migration Discovery & Assessment Lead** who will play a key role in large-scale enterprise transformation and cloud migration initiatives. This role is critical in shaping successful migration programs by creating clarity, structure, and direction at the very start of the journey.
  

As Migration Discovery & Assessment Lead, you will be responsible for understanding complex enterprise IT landscapes, translating data into insights, and turning those insights into clear, executable migration roadmaps that align technology with business goals.
  

  

**You will:**
  

* Lead discovery and assessment activities for large enterprise environments, covering applications, infrastructure, platforms, and dependencies
* Collect, structure, and analyze large volumes of data from multiple sources such as CMDBs, discovery tools, workshops, and interviews
* Assess on premises and cloud environments and define target-state migration strategies
* Design and present migration roadmaps, including migration waves, dependencies, risks, recommended migration approaches, and associated cost and TCO considerations
* Translate technical findings into clear, decision-ready proposals, including TCO analysis and migration business cases, for architects, IT leadership, and business stakeholders
* Act as a trusted advisor to clients, helping them understand what to migrate, how to migrate, and in which order
* Collaborate with architects, engineers, and business representatives to ensure alignment between technical feasibility, financial viability, and business priorities

**Your profile**
  

We are looking for someone who combines strong analytical skills, broad technical knowledge, and excellent communication abilities.
  

  

**You also have:**
  

* Proven experience leading discovery and assessment phases in enterprise migration projects
* Strong background in on premises infrastructure (data centers, servers, networks, storage, virtualization, operating systems)
* Solid experience with public cloud platforms (Azure, AWS and/or GCP) and cloud migration concepts
* Hands-on experience with one or more migration and discovery tools, such as Azure Migrate, AWS Application Discovery Service, AWS Application Migration Service (MGN), Cast Highlight, Cast Imaging, Cloudamize, and Txture
* Ability to work with complex data sets and transform them into structured insights and actionable recommendations
* Full working proficiency in both Swedish and English

  

**What we believe you will enjoy about working here**
  

* A challenging career in a vibrant environment with access to extensive training, a global network of experts, and an innovative culture that promotes learning, growth, and shared success
* Work on large, business-critical transformation programs, helping organizations modernize their IT landscapes and move confidently toward the future
* A company culture driven by core values: Honesty, Trust, Freedom, Team Spirit, Modesty, Fun, and Boldness
* A strong commitment to inclusion, diversity, and supporting the whole person

  

**Ready to make it real? Explore opportunities with CIS and shape the future of digital infrastructure.**  

  

*As part of our recruitment process, identity verification will be conducted through inspection of your ID card, and background checks may be carried out as required.*
  

*#LI-JF1*
Capgemini is an AI-powered global business and technology transformation partner, delivering tangible business value. We imagine the future of organizations and make it real with AI, technology and people. With our strong heritage of nearly 60 years, we are a responsible and diverse group of 420,000 team members in more than 50 countries. We deliver end-to-end services and solutions with our deep industry expertise and strong partner ecosystem, leveraging our capabilities across strategy, technology, design, engineering and business operations. The Group reported 2024 global revenues of €22.1 billion.
  

Make it real | www.capgemini.com

title: 6G Product Planner
description:
Overview:

Keysight is at the forefront of technology innovation, delivering breakthroughs and trusted insights in electronic design, simulation, prototyping, test, manufacturing, and optimization. Our ~16,800 employees create world-class solutions in communications, 5G, automotive, energy, quantum, aerospace, defense, and semiconductor markets for customers in over 100 countries. Learn more about what we do.
Our award-winning culture embraces a bold vision of where technology can take us and a passion for tackling challenging problems with industry-first solutions. We believe that when people feel a sense of belonging, they can be more creative, innovative, and thrive at all points in their careers.  

This role will be that of a senior technical leader responsible for driving innovation and influence in shaping the **product strategy and roadmap** for next-generation wireless protocol test solutions for 6G, with focus on AI, Agentic AI, and other forward-looking areas. This role envisions technology foresight, understanding industry trends and market-driven product planning to ensure industry-leading test and measurement offerings.
Responsibilities:
**Product Planning & Strategy*** Cultivate a bird’s eye view and perspective of the industry trends, looking into the 3-5 year horizons, and mapping those insights into broad based product strategy
* Influence **product roadmap** for wireless protocol test platforms (UE, network, and core testing)
* Partner with R&D to prioritize features based on **technical feasibility and business impact**
* Translate technology priorities into concrete technical requirements

**Technical Architecture & Innovation*** Provide system-level architecture guidance for protocol test solutions
* Ensure alignment of products with evolving wireless technologies and deployments
* Drive innovation in:
* + AI-native workflows in protocol validation, network optimization, and adjacent areas, both in commercial solutions as well as internal toolchains
	+ Protocol emulation and simulation
	+ End-to-end network testing
	+ Cloud-native and virtualized test solutions
* Contribute to patents, whitepapers, and technical publications

**Cross-Functional Leadership*** Collaborate with:
* + R&D (feature definition and implementation)
	+ Marketing (positioning and messaging)
	+ Sales (customer engagement and solution support)
* Mentor senior engineers and act as a technical authority within the organization
* Support key customer engagements with deep technical insights

Qualifications: **Education*** Master’s or PhD in Electrical Engineering, Telecommunications, or related field

**Experience*** 12+ years in wireless communications, with strong focus on:
* + 3GPP standardization contributions
	+ Wireless protocol development or testing
* Experience in **product planning or technical product management**

**Technical Expertise*** Deep knowledge of:
* + 5G NR protocol stack (RRC, MAC, PHY interactions)
	+ Leading 6G technology candidates and trends
	+ LTE and legacy technologies
	+ Core network signaling (5GC, EPC)
* Strong understanding of:
* + Conformance and interoperability testing
	+ Test methodologies and automation
* Familiarity with:
* + Network simulators, protocol analyzers, and lab test systems

**Business & Strategic Skills*** Ability to connect standards evolution to **commercial product opportunities**
* Strong analytical skills for **market and competitive analysis**
* Experience defining product requirements and influencing roadmap decisions

**Soft Skills*** Excellent communication and presentation skills
* Ability to influence without authority across global teams
* Strong industry presence and credibility

**Preferred Qualifications*** Active rapporteur or leadership role within 3GPP
* Experience with **5G-Advanced or early 6G research**
* Background in test and measurement industry
* Exposure to business strategy frameworks

  

Careers Privacy Statement\*\*\*Keysight is an Equal Opportunity Employer.\*\*\*

title: Health & Safety Manager, Data Center Health and Safety
description:
**DESCRIPTION**
---------------


Join the world class safety culture at Amazon Web Services (AWS) and be part of supporting the largest cloud computing infrastructure team as a Health and Safety Manager. As the Health and Safety Manager you will own the Health and Safety function of your respective area. You will be responsible for improving the health and safety of our data centers, advancing safety culture, and protecting our employees. Each day you will work with our data center engineers, the people who make the cloud go. They are working with electrical switching equipment, large scale air handling mechanical systems, and critical IT gear.
  

  

You will work closely with experts in security, construction, operations, logistics, compliance, corporate counsel, and human resources to further the global health safety program. You will partner with employees as well as contractors to achieve health and safety goals and improve our already strong safety culture. As AWS increases data capacity you will partner with construction managers to ensure risks are identified and mitigated prior to live operations. You will work in a fast-paced environment where our operations are rapidly changing, advocating safe changes in the face of new infrastructure, new tools, and new products/processes.
  

  

This role is for an experienced Safety Specialist or Safety Engineer. The role's prime responsibility will be to support the safety operations for Data Centers. You will be part of a regional and global team that works with internal and external partners to maintain and enhance the safety of operations. Close collaboration with onsite physical security, vendors, compliance officers, risk management, and fire/life safety systems is required. Additionally, you will work with employees and contractors to achieve specific safety objectives.
  

  

You must be an effective communicator, sending clear, concise and consistent messages verbally, in written form, and/or public speaking forums. You will advise, instruct and train company personnel on safety policies and procedures, as well as assist the teams in incorporating and improving our safety standards at their sites.
  

  

You will report to the Regional Safety Manager – EMEA and be responsible for continuing to enhance the safety of operations and construction. Some domestic and international travel is required.
  

  

Key job responsibilities  

* Implement safety standards, policies and best practices.
* Provision and Management of Training: Induction, Refresher Training as required, develop and deliver training/presentations to support achievement of H&S objectives. Ensure all H&S training requirements are fulfilled.
* Collaborate with vendors (General Contractors, sub-contractors, Emergency Services) as they relate to operations and construction.


•Ensure compliance and implementation of global safety programs and local regulatory legislation. •Develop and review safety plans for on-site emergencies, special events and other unique safety situations.  

* Perform safety data analysis and create bench marking.
* Ensure safety record keeping and provide accurate reporting and metrics to support business safety initiatives.


•Consult and work with compliance teams on safety regulations interpretations and investigations. •Develop and implement solutions to eliminate exposure to risks and prevent injury.  

•Adapt to fast-paced environments with changing circumstances, direction, strategy and deadlines. •Able to work as part of a team and provide safety support to regions outside of local cluster when required.  

* Other duties as assigned.

  

About the team  

We’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.
  

  

You’ll join a diverse team of engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers.
**BASIC QUALIFICATIONS**
------------------------

* Minimum 5+ years of field experience in in a Health & Safety role within operational environments.
* Fluent English language skill required (written and verbal communication).
* Valid driver's license.
* Ability to work on-site at office and Data Center locations.

**PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS**
----------------------------

* Considerable knowledge of local safety and health regulatory laws and practices and workplace safety.
* Experience / knowledge of implementing safety programs, processes, and procedural documentation.
* Data center, manufacturing, construction, or similar industry safety experience with mix of full-time employees and third-party contractors.

  

Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates.  

  

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

title: Client Director Nordics
description:
This is an incredible opportunity to be part of a company that has been at the forefront of AI and high-performance data storage innovation for over two decades. DataDirect Networks (DDN) is a global market leader renowned for powering many of the world's most demanding AI data centers, in industries ranging from life sciences and healthcare to financial services, autonomous cars, Government, academia, research and manufacturing. **"DDN's A3I solutions are transforming the landscape of AI infrastructure." – IDC** ***“The real differentiator is DDN. I never hesitate to recommend DDN. DDN is the de facto name for AI Storage in high performance environments” - Marc Hamilton, VP, Solutions Architecture & Engineering | NVIDIA***  

DDN is the global leader in AI and multi-cloud data management at scale. Our cutting-edge data intelligence platform is designed to accelerate AI workloads, enabling organizations to extract maximum value from their data. With a proven track record of performance, reliability, and scalability, DDN empowers businesses to tackle the most challenging AI and data-intensive workloads with confidence.  

Our success is driven by our unwavering commitment to innovation, customer-centricity, and a team of passionate professionals who bring their expertise and dedication to every project. This is a chance to make a significant impact at a company that is shaping the future of AI and data management.  

Our commitment to innovation, customer success, and market leadership makes this an exciting and rewarding role for a driven professional looking to make a lasting impact in the world of AI and data storage.  

DDN is seeking a **Client Director – Strategic AI Infrastructure** to drive revenue growth in the **Nordics** within our Artificial Intelligence business unit. This is a **quota-carrying role** responsible for developing strategic enterprise opportunities and expanding DDN’s presence within organizations building large-scale AI and high-performance computing environments.

  

In this role, the Account Executive will operate as a **Strategic AI Client Director**, partnering with enterprise customers, research institutions, and cloud organizations to deliver high-performance data infrastructure solutions that support advanced AI workloads.


The successful candidate will work closely with internal engineering teams, solutions architects, and channel partners to design and deliver complex AI infrastructure solutions supporting **GPU computing, large-scale model training, and data-intensive workloads**.

**Key Responsibilities*** Act as a **Strategic AI Client Director** within the assigned Nordics region, driving adoption of DDN’s AI infrastructure solutions across enterprise and research organizations.
* Develop and execute **strategic territory and account plans** targeting organizations building large-scale AI and high-performance computing environments.
* Identify and qualify new opportunities across industries including **financial services, healthcare, telecommunications, technology, research institutions, and large enterprise organizations**.
* Build trusted relationships with **senior technical leaders, AI infrastructure teams, and executive decision-makers** within target accounts.
* Partner with solutions architects and engineering teams to design and present **AI infrastructure and high-performance data storage solutions**.
* Deliver compelling presentations, product demonstrations, and proposals showcasing the value of DDN’s AI-optimized storage platforms.
* Manage the full enterprise sales cycle including **pipeline development, forecasting, contract negotiation, and deal closure**.
* Develop strong relationships with **channel partners, system integrators, OEM partners, and distributors** to expand market reach.
* Stay current on market trends related to **AI infrastructure, GPU computing, large-scale model training, and high-performance data environments**.
* Represent DDN at industry events, conferences, and customer engagements to generate new opportunities and strengthen market presence.

**Required Qualifications*** Bachelor’s degree or equivalent professional experience.
* 8+ years of experience in **enterprise technology sales**, preferably within storage, compute, networking, or infrastructure platforms.
* Demonstrated experience selling **AI infrastructure, HPC solutions, cloud platforms, or enterprise data center technologies**.
* Proven track record of **meeting or exceeding sales quotas** in enterprise technology environments.
* Experience managing **complex enterprise sales cycles** involving technical buyers and executive stakeholders.
* Ability to travel within the East Coast territory as needed.

**Preferred Experience*** Experience selling **AI infrastructure, GPU clusters, or high-performance computing platforms**.
* Familiarity with the AI ecosystem including technologies from **NVIDIA, Intel, AMD, Cerebras, Graphcore, or Google TPU**.
* Knowledge of enterprise storage technologies including **SAN, NAS, object storage, and parallel file systems**.
* Experience working with **channel partners, system integrators, and distributor ecosystems**.
* Familiarity with technologies such as **Kubernetes, OpenStack, and large-scale AI data center environments**.

  



**Why Join DDN**
At DDN, you will work at the forefront of AI infrastructure innovation, helping organizations solve some of the most demanding data challenges in the world. Our teams collaborate across engineering, sales, and customer success to deliver cutting-edge technology that powers the next generation of AI breakthroughs.

  

DDN has a very strong orientation towards these 4 characteristics and any successful employee will demonstrate these capabilities: **Self-Starter -** Takes independent action to identify and solve problems. Seeks out relevant information needed to make decisions. Gets involved with new initiatives.**Success/Achievement Orientation -** Delivers quality results consistently. Targets, achieves (or exceeds) measurable results. Sets challenging goals, focuses on critical priorities, and is accountable.**Problem Solving -** Recognizes problems and responds with a systematic assessment that identifies and addresses cause of issue. Practical, realistic, and resourceful.**Innovative -** Builds and improves key business processes that enhance the effectiveness of DDN. Generates new ideas, challenges the status quo, and solves problems creatively. **DataDirect Networks, Inc. is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action employer.** All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity, gender expression, transgender, sex stereotyping, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, protected Veteran Status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable federal, state, or local law.

title: Site Reliability Engineer
description:
**About Us**
 Visa is a world leader in payments technology, facilitating transactions between consumers, merchants, financial institutions and government entities across more than 200 countries and territories, dedicated to uplifting everyone, everywhere by being the best way to pay and be paid.
   

  

 At Visa, you'll have the opportunity to create impact at scale — tackling meaningful challenges, growing your skills and seeing your contributions impact lives around the world.
   

  

 Join Visa and do work that matters – to you, to your community, and to the world. Progress starts with you.
   

  

**Job Description**
 We are seeking a Site Reliability Engineer (SRE) to join Visa Open Banking (VOB), delivering open banking solutions as part of Visa.
   

  

 Visa Open Banking processes billions of events, enabling data-driven decisions for clients and internal stakeholders.
   

  

 As an SRE in the Infrastructure & Tooling area, you will play a key role in ensuring our platforms are reliable, scalable, secure, and easy to use, while empowering engineering teams with self-service tools and automation.
   

  

 This role spans both cloud infrastructure (AWS, Kubernetes, runtime) and engineering productivity platforms (CI/CD, developer portals, observability), focusing on improving developer experience and overall system resilience.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Build and operate reliable, scalable, and secure infrastructure platforms across AWS and Kubernetes
* Develop and maintain self-service tooling and platforms to enable teams to deploy and operate services independently
* Improve CI/CD pipelines, developer experience, and platform usability
* Drive observability strategies across logs, metrics, and tracing
* Automate provisioning, deployment, and scaling using scripting (Python, Go, Bash, etc.)
* Support engineering teams with best practices in reliability, performance, and cost optimisation
* Collaborate with stakeholders to ensure platforms align with evolving use cases and requirements
* Contribute to architecture, standards, and platform designs for large-scale distributed systems
* Act as a champion for automation, platform engineering, and operational excellence


 This is a hybrid position. Expectation of days in office will be confirmed by your hiring manager.
   

  

**Qualifications**
 Basic Qualifications:
   

  

* Relevant work experience and a Bachelor's degree
* Experience working with cloud platforms (AWS or similar) and container orchestration (Kubernetes/EKS)
* Solid understanding of CI/CD pipelines and modern software delivery practices
* Experience with infrastructure as code and automation tools
* Working knowledge of monitoring and observability tools (e.g. metrics, logs, tracing)
* Programming or scripting skills (e.g. Python, Go, Bash)
* Understanding of distributed systems, reliability, and scalability principles
* Strong collaboration and communication skills


**Preferred Qualifications**
* Experience building or operating developer platforms (e.g. Backstage, internal portals, self-service tooling)
* Experience with observability platforms such as Datadog
* Knowledge of platform engineering and developer experience practices
* Experience with large-scale, event-driven systems
* Understanding of cost optimisation and operational efficiency in cloud environments
* Experience influencing architecture decisions and engineering standards
* Familiarity with security and compliance requirements in cloud environments


**Visa is an EEO Employer**
 Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability or protected veteran status. Visa will also consider for employment qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with EEOC guidelines and applicable local law.

title: Machine Learning Engineer
description:
**Assignment description** 




 We are looking for a skilled Machine Learning Engineer to join one of our Supply Chain product teams. In this role, you will contribute to the development, maintenance, and continuous improvement of AI/ML solutions that support business-critical processes. You will work closely with cross-functional teams in an agile environment, helping to transform data into scalable and impactful solutions.
 



  





**Qualifications** 




 Applicable working experience or a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Machine Learning, or a related technical field.
 



 Solid understanding of software development principles, algorithms, data structures, and design patterns.
 



 Deep knowledge of Python programming, as well as familiarity with frameworks, programming languages, and paradigms relevant to AI/ML development.
 



 Solid understanding of common AI/ML algorithms and paradigms.
 



 Familiarity with Agile development methodologies.
 



 Experience with version control systems such as Git and MLOps.
 



 Experience with Google Cloud Platform and cloud-based development.
 



  





**Role-Specific Skills** 




 Ability to analyse, troubleshoot, and resolve issues of varying complexity in existing AI/ML solutions.
 



 Good understanding of the different phases of the AI/ML development lifecycle and the ability to contribute throughout the process.
 



 Understanding of software testing methodologies and the ability to recommend and implement appropriate testing approaches.
 



 Ability to communicate technical concepts clearly, including writing user stories, and collaborate effectively with both team members and stakeholders to ensure transparency and alignment.
 



 Good understanding of coding standards, best practices, and design patterns, with the ability to apply them appropriately to solve complex problems while improving maintainability and performance.
 



 Possess a broad understanding of different technology stacks and tools, along with the ability to make informed implementation decisions aligned with business requirements and organizational standards.
 



 Ability to independently address technical challenges while recognizing when collaboration or support is needed to achieve the best outcome.
 



 This is an excellent opportunity for a hands-on Machine Learning Engineer who enjoys solving complex challenges, working with modern cloud technologies, and contributing to the development of scalable AI/ML solutions in a collaborative environment.
 



  





**Requirements** 




**Must have:** 




 Applicable working experience or a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Machine Learning, or a related technical field.
 



 Solid understanding of software development principles, algorithms, data structures, and design patterns.
 



 Deep knowledge of Python programming, as well as familiarity with frameworks, programming languages, and paradigms relevant to AI/ML development.
 



 Solid understanding of common AI/ML algorithms and paradigms.
 



 Familiarity with Agile development methodologies.
 



 Experience with version control systems such as Git and MLOps.
 



 Experience with Google Cloud Platform and cloud-based development.
 



 Required cloud certification: GCP

title: IT Consultant - Stockholm | BCG Platinion
description:
Who We Are
   

  

 Do you want to turn the challenges of digitalization into the solutions of the future? Join BCG Platinion in tackling the core IT issues of leading global companies!
   

  

 Deploy customized platforms, architectures, strategies, and organizations to leverage the digital potential of our customers and drive true innovation, with state-of-the-art tools such as cloud computing, AI, blockchain, microservices, and containerization. Add your individual strengths to our unique team, always there to support you. Together with Boston Consulting Group and our clients, we turn digital visions into reality.
   

  

 What You'll Do
   

  

 For our Stockholm office, we are looking for IT Consultants who will collaborate with colleagues from BCG Platinion and/or the Boston Consulting Group on local and international client projects!
   

  

 What You'll Bring
   

  

**You can help us make a difference if you**
* have a degree in computer science, informatics, business with focus on IT, or a related field
* gathered approx. two years of relevant experience in IT consulting, either in part- or full time roles
* are no stranger to the buzzwords machine learning, cyber resilience, DevOps, agile, AI, and robotics
* enjoy tackling complex digital challenges and are enthusiastic about developing and sharing solutions
* feel comfortable in interdisciplinary teams and global contexts and are flexible and willing to travel
* are excellent at understanding and communicating complex topics in English


 Who You'll Work With
   

  

**Shape the world of tomorrow**
* Shape the digital future of globally leading companies in an individual client context with overarching technical solutions
* Develop groundbreaking projects by enabling your clients to effectively use agile methods and modern approaches in the context of strategic IT implementation
* Smooth the path for tailored, future-oriented customer journeys based on precise analyses of the individual situation and with current market developments and trends such as machine learning in mind
* Design and optimize unique IT architectures within the framework of enterprise solution requirements, modern custom software developments, and sustainable digital solutions, including cyber resilience


 Additional info
   

  

**How To Grow With Us**
 We guarantee constant development incentives in an open and rewarding work environment! Together with a dedicated team of talented colleagues, you get to work on new challenges every day. Individual training offers and a culture of open feedback will provide the framework needed to explore and perfect personal strengths. Our modern structures allow focusing on the essence of our work - with easy travel booking, an excellent back-office support and comprehensive Knowledge Center. With our regular office Fridays and legendary events, we nurture a strong and close team network beyond our case work.
   

  

*#BCGPlatinion*
 Boston Consulting Group is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, age, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity / expression, national origin, disability, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected under national, provincial, or local law, where applicable, and those with criminal histories will be considered in a manner consistent with applicable state and local laws.
   

  

 BCG is an E - Verify Employer. Click here for more information on E-Verify.

title: VP Group IT & Tech Procurement
description:
At Viaplay Group, we entertain millions of people every day through our streaming services, radio networks, and TV channels. We believe in the power of content not just as a way of telling stories and touching lives, but also expanding worlds.
   

  

 We’re looking for the best people to join us on our journey. Right now, we’re searching for a
 **VP Group IT & Tech Procurement** 
 in our
 **Stockholm** 
 office – are you ready to hit play on an exciting career change?
   

  

**The Role**
 As the new VP of Group IT & Tech Procurement, you will take full ownership and responsibility of an efficient development of the internal IT environment, technical tools & systems - ensuring that Viaplay Group employees are equipped with the tools necessary to maximize productivity, as well as overseeing the portfolio of vendor agreements and vendor relations across the entire Product, Data & Tech organization. You will bring some fresh energy and help figure out how AI can actually improve day-to-day operations.
   

  

 You will lead a talented, distributed team of approx 13 talented people across Sweden, Denmark, and the UK. You will be the architect behind securing a smart IT system landscape and integrations which maximizes the productivity of their employees, whilst also securing a proactive procurement strategy and stakeholder management that ensures that they stay ahead of the curve.
   

  

 You’ll report directly to the EVP, CTO & CPO and join the PD&T leadership team that covers everything from Engineering and Data to Product and Monetization.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Leading Leaders: Lead and motivate skilled and experienced managers using a context-driven and transformational leadership style.
* Group IT: Spanning across IT Operations, Security and Productivity, your responsibility is to drive transformation in order to enable the organization to become even more productive, whilst maintaining a cost efficient platform.
* Tools and systems: Own the toolkit (Office IT, Teams, Jira, Airtable, etc.) and make sure these tools actually help people work better, not just sit there.
* Tech Procurement: Drive a large agreement portfolio, managing high-value partnerships with the likes of Cloud platform vendors, SAAS vendors, CDNs, satellite providers etc.
* Innovation & AI: Act as a "mind opener." We want you to challenge the "we've always done it this way" mentality and show how AI and new tech can simplify our lives.
* Smart Governance and stakeholder management: Find that sweet spot where they have the right guard rails in place without IT becoming a "blocker" for the rest of the business.


**What We’re Looking For**
 We’re looking for someone open minded, inquisitive and self-driven. You’ve got the seniority to command a room but the pragmatism to keep things simple. You thrive in an environment where strategies might shift, and you’re comfortable pivoting when they do.
   

  

**We Think You Have**
* A strong leadership presence with the ability to inspire, develop, and guide teams through change. Comfortable being strategic as well as handson and an accomplished leader of leaders.
* Solid experience from running system integration projects, running internal IT operations and procurement and negotiation. Ideally from several companies, giving you a broad perspective and a sense of what good looks like.
* A pragmatic mindset to prioritize delivery and customer experience over rigid bureaucracy.
* A collaborative spirit, eager to share knowledge and expertise across teams and departments.


 What we offer you
   

  

* We’ve got you covered! 30 days of paid vacation every year, an attractive pension and insurance scheme, and generous parental leave pay lift.
* Your wellbeing matters. We provide you with various wellbeing initiatives including wellness allowance.
* Flexibility that works. We follow a hybrid model with at least three days a week in the office, giving you the best mix of collaboration and focus time.
* A safe space to grow and up-skill. Our learning culture puts you in the driver’s seat of your own development.
* An innovative environment with Hack Days once a year. This week-long initiative allows you to think outside the box and deliver creative, technical solutions that (more often than not) go on to be implemented, either in our product or our ways of working.
* Entertainment is what we love, and entertainment is what we do. So, unlimited access to Viaplay seems only fair for you to get to know the product – including serier & viewing events, new release movie rentals, linear channels and more.


 If you’re ready to lead a passionate, talented team in an exciting and fast-paced industry, we’d love to hear from you.
   

  

***Viaplay is collaborating with Safemind as a recruitment partner.***

title: Client Director/Marketing Advisor
description:
**OM HOLY COMMS** 




  





 Holy Comms är en snabbväxande kommunikationsbyrå i Stockholm. Vi definierar oss som en Marketing as a Service-byrå (MaaS) och lägger stor vikt vid att skapa flexibla, skräddarsydda och abonnemangsbaserade lösningar för våra kunder. Vi jobbar bland annat med aktörer som Gårdschips, MSAB, QleanAir och ABG – men även ett stort antal innovativa startups. Vi är övertygade om att digitaliseringen har bidragit till att alla företag idag, inte bara de allra största, har ett snabbväxande behov av marknadsföring.
 



  





 Holy Comms erbjudande innefattar ett kluster av tjänster och områden som PR, content marketing, performance film och foto, kampanjer, sociala medier och strategi, vilket gör vårt erbjudande både brett och spetsigt, eller som vi har definerat det "Holystic". Vi tror på långsiktiga kundrelationer och lägger stor vikt vid att alltid leverera lite bättre än sist. Vi drivs av våra tre värdeord flexibilitet, transparens och snällhet.
 



  





 Att välja Holy Comms innebär precis vad du vill att det ska innebära. Vi är en fullservicebyrå, absolut, men vi är också en partner som brinner för att hjälpa dig att nå dina drömmar. För vi vill att alla som har en dröm och ett driv ska ha möjligheten att skapa fantastisk kommunikation. Därför utgår vår modell från flexibla månadsabonnemang med transparent prissättning. Vi är under snabb tillväxt, är 35 personer och sitter just nu i centrala Stockholm på Tegnergatan.
 



  





**OM ROLLEN** 




  





 Denna roll utgör en mycket viktigt del i Holy Comms erbjudande till våra kunder. I rollen agerar du som våra kunders strategiska och operativa rådgivare tillsammans med övriga kollegor på Holy Comms. vi på Holy Comms erbjuder våra kunder att skräddarsy sina kommunikations och marknadstjänster där behoven månad för månad kan skilja sig åt.
 



  





 Vi har experter brett inom marknad och erbjuder våra kunder allt i från effektiv content till marknadsstrategier. I denna roll kommer du att fylla rollen som motsvarar en marknadsansvarig där du tillsammans med våra kollegor säkrar att alla våra leveranser håller en röd tråd och skapar mesta möjliga värde för våra kunder. Alla våra kunder har olika behov och har olika mognadsgrad och syfte med sin kommunikation. Vissa har en egen marknadsavdelning där vi
 



 är en stöttande funktion, vissa kunder har vi ett helhetsansvar då vi erbjuder en komplett marknadsavdelning.
 



  





 Denna roll utgör navet i Holy Comms erbjudande där ansvarar för att våra kunder erbjuds de tjänster som de behöver för att nå sina mål. Tillsammans med creatives och strateg ansvar du för att kunden får ut mesta möjliga av sitt abonnemang och för Holy Comms skapar ett reelt värde för kunden. 
 



  





 I denna roll har du ett helhetsansvar för dina kunders abonnemang. Du ansvarar för alla delar av projektets livscykel: uppstart, strategi, produktion, distribution, mätning och optimering. Vi är en startup, därför är det viktigt att du kan hantera hela kedjan – från uppstart av en helt ny kund till att förädla långvariga samarbeten och relationer.
 



  
 



 Du kommer att jobba med kunder som aldrig förut gjort något online likväl som att addera det där lilla extra till marknadsavdelningar i framkant. Våra kunder verkar inom ett stort antal olika branscher – från retail till fintech. Det är därför viktigt att du tycker att det är roligt med mixen, och att sätta dig in i nya områden.
 



  





 Några av dina arbetsuppgifter innefattar:
 



  




* Hantera dina kunders abonnemang och vara deras kontaktperson – du ansvarar för projektens innehåll, tidplan och leverans
* Beställa och projektleda produktioner internt från kollegorna på vår Creative-avdelning som deltar i dina kundteam
* Löpande förbättra och förädla varje kunds samarbete
* Pitcha in nya idéer till dina kunder
* Ansvara för att produktion och distribution genomförs i dina kundprojekt



  





**VEM ÄR DU** 




  




* Du är kundorienterad och sätter kundernas framgång och leverans i centrum
* Du har kanske erfarenhet av arbete som marketing manager eller haft en annan liknande roll på en marknads eller kommunikationsavdelning. Givetvis är byråerfarenhet extra meriterande.
* Du har god vana vid kundansvar och är självgående i kundrelationen
* Du är innovativ och har inte brist på idéer – det kommer naturligt för dig att löpande se hur kundprojekten kan utvecklas
* Du är ansvarsfull, har koll på både helhet och detaljer och skulle aldrig missa en deadline
* Du är en people person som har lätt för att bygga goda relationer
* Du är tech-savvy och håller dig uppdaterad kring nya tekniker, kanaler och metoder inom digital kommunikation – du behöver inte kunna allt, din inställning till att lära dig nytt är viktigast
* Du är van vid att leda och koordinera (andra) kreatörer
* Du är snabb, driven och gillar ett högt tempo, men tummar för den sakens skull inte på kvaliteten
* Du delar vår syn om att kommunikationsbranschen skulle må bra av en dos förändring



  





 Vi på Holy Comms tror på att vara snäll, flexibel och transparent och ser att du delar de värderingarna. Redan från start har Holy Comms varit ett enkelt bolag att arbeta med, vi finns där för våra kunder och vi tycker det är viktigare att våra kunder når sina mål än att vi vinner ett guldägg. Vi är inte svåra eller diffusa, för vi vill att alla som har en dröm och ett driv ska ha möjligheten att skapa fantastisk kommunikation.
 



  





 .Är det dig vi söker? Skicka in din ansökan redan idag, urval och intervjuer sker löpande. CV och personligt brev mailas till fredrik@holycomms.se

title: Data Analytics Engineer
description:
Are you ready to be a key contributor within the Data & Analytics Engineering department at NOBA Bank Group? Your focus will be on advancing analytics capabilities, optimizing data workflows, driving innovation in the cloud, and unlocking the full potential of the organization’s most valuable asset – data.
   

  

**About The Role**
 The Data & Analytics Engineering department comprises specialized teams focused on Data Infrastructure Engineering, Analytics Engineering, and Data Management.
   

  

 As a Data Analytics Engineer, you'll play a central role in building and optimizing the organization's supporting analytics layer, collaborating across the bank. This role offers broad opportunities to grow and make a tangible impact!
   

  

 We offer a hybrid working model with two common in-office days. While we value flexibility, we also prioritize collaboration, team-building, and enjoying fikas and after-work gatherings together!
   

  

**Main Tasks**
* Design and implement efficient data models, focusing on analytics workflows and end-user usability.
* Build robust and scalable data pipelines to support business intelligence and analytics needs.
* Collaborate closely with internal stakeholders, such as Risk, Finance, and IT, to ensure data solutions align with business needs.
* Ensure data quality, security, and compliance throughout the analytics lifecycle.
* Drive technical improvements, introducing modern tools and processes to enhance analytics capabilities.
* Partner with other engineers to align transformation processes for analytics purposes.


**We Are Looking For Someone Who**
 Enjoys working with data & analytics engineering, modern data practices, and creating insights that drive decisions. We believe you are a detail-oriented and proactive person who thrives in a high pace enviroment and enjoy learning new technologies. You approach challenges with a “can-do” attitude and excel in analytical thinking and problem-solving.
   

  

**For This Role You’ll Need**
* Work experience in a similar role within data engineering or analytics engineering
* An academic degree related to analytics engineering, data engineering, or similar fields.
* Proficiency in ETL processes, designing analytics-ready datasets, and ensuring clean and usable data for stakeholders.
* Proficiency in Databricks, dbt (SQL), python and related reporting tools and languages.
* Experience with Power BI or similar BI tools.
* Strong English skills, with proficiency in Swedish and/or Norwegian.


**It’s a Plus If You Have**
* Experience with Microsoft’s data stack (Azure, SQL, and other cloud services).
* Experience working with on-prem solutions.
* A background in data roles within the financial sector.
* Knowledge of Agile and Scrum methodologies.


**Our Offer To You**
* Freedom with Responsibility: Flexible working arrangements to suit your work style.
* Career Development: Opportunities to grow and develop in your role.
* Feedback Culture: A supportive environment that encourages open communication.
* Coaching Leadership: Managers who guide and support your career journey.
* A Caring Company Culture: We genuinely care about each other!


 In addition to this, it is important to us that you recognize yourself in our values:
 **we are wholehearted, we collaborate and we take the lead.**
**Have you heard of NOBAVERSE?**
 In our NOBAVERSE, we value openness, trust, and diversity. Here, you can influence your own development and have fun along the way to achieving our goals – all in a workplace that’s a little extra
 *bubbly* 
 ! ✨
   

  

 Read more about our employee value proposition on our careers page.
   

  

**Read more about how our recruitment process works in the** 
**FAQ** 
**.**
 We care for your privacy and, with reference to the GDPR, would like to ask you not to include any sensitive personal data in your application, for examples information about ethnic origin, political opinions, religious beliefs or information about your health.
   

  

**Read more about how we handle your personal data** 
**here** 
**.**
 If you have any questions or concerns about the position, please contact the responsible recruiter.
   

  

 We look forward to receiving your application! 🙏✨

title: Head of Safety - Autonomous Technologies
description:
Born autonomous. Born electric.
   

  

 Since 2016, our technology has connected global communities with the things they need – and all without the emissions. We’re made up of experts in multiple fields and that’s how we’ve developed a new way to move goods. This is technology purpose-built for the industry. This is safer, cleaner, and more reliable movement.
   

  

 We are Einride and this is how we build a better future.
   

  

 Want to be part of transforming road freight – for good? Einride is showing the world a new way to move, based on the latest digital, electric and autonomous technologies. Through freight capacity as a service, we enable businesses around the world to accelerate towards their sustainability goals.
   

  

 Founded in 2016, Einride became the first company in the world to deploy a cab-less autonomous electric vehicle on a public road (Sweden, 2019). In 2022, we were the first to successfully operate such a vehicle on a US public road. Today our award-winning technology has been launched across 8 countries (and counting). Our clients are some of the world’s biggest shippers, including Fortune 500 companies. We are also operating Sweden’s largest truck dedicated public charging network and counting.
   

  

 As Head of Safety, you will lead a global team responsible for all aspects of safety of Einride’s autonomous business—including ensuring safety of our autonomous vehicles through development and operation, safety of our AI-based automated driving system, human factors, remote operations, and monitoring and maintaining safety across our globally deployed fleet. You will partner closely with engineering, product, operations, global affairs, and external authorities, auditors and customers to ensure our systems are safe in operation, verifiable, and aligned with global standards and regulations in all markets where we operate our vehicles.
   

  

 The strategic component to this role is also substantial; you will set the long-term strategy for safety and establish the frameworks, structures, and arguments required for commercial-scale autonomy, through the safety case and safety management system for our organization.
   

  

**You Will**
* Lead the design, implementation, and continual improvement of ERA’s Safety Management System (SMS), aligned with global best practices used in aviation, automotive, and other safety-critical industries. This includes defining safety policies and objectives, risk management, safety assurance, safety promotion and safety culture.
* Own the end-to-end safety case for autonomous operations, aligned with global standards such as UL 4600, ISO 5083 and relevant current and emerging regulations in UNECE markets, US, and GCC markets.
* Establish internal and external safety governance structures, including review boards, supporting processes, and escalation pathways to ensure consistent decision-making across all markets.
* Partner with hardware, software and system engineering teams to define high-level safety requirements across the ADS stack, vehicle platform, remote operations, cloud systems, AI/ML components, and operating environments
* Oversee the software release safety assessment process to independently validate and confirm readiness of each release from our high-velocity software machine, ensuring an acceptable level of risk is achieved and key assets and evidences conform to technical standards including ISO 26262, ISO 21448, ISO 21434, ISO 8800 and more.
* Ensure that safety relevant architecture, redundancy concepts, ODD definitions, and fallback strategies are robust, auditable, and ready for regulatory review.
* Define operational procedures, operator requirements, training and certification programs, and safety monitoring functions.
* Oversee safety incident response program, field issue analysis and escalation, and continuous fleet operational monitoring to deliver improvement through deployment.
* Scope, plan, and direct the execution of safety audits and third party assessments in service of unlocking new permits, markets and customer deployments


**We Expect You To**
* Have 15+ years experience in a highly-regulated, safety-critical industry (automotive, aviation, aerospace, robotics, nuclear, rail, or similar).
* Have 10+ years of experience in automated systems or autonomous vehicles, including hands-on leadership in engineering development.
* Bring deep expertise in safety management systems, and has implemented or operated SMS structures at scale.
* Have direct experience operating or supporting large, complex, distributed fleets in real-world environments.
* Be highly proficient with ISO 26262, ISO 21448, ISO 8800, ISO 5083, UL 4600, and other AV-specific safety standards and methods.
* Be familiar with global regulatory frameworks, including UNECE R155 & R156, and U.S. FMVSS implications for ADS deployments.
* Have a proven track record of leading high-performing global teams, ideally across engineering and operations.


 This is a full-time position based in Stockholm, Gothenburg, or the US (with frequent travel to Austin). You will be part of a truly diverse, high performing team with a common passion for sustainability and making things happen in an innovative way. We recommend that you submit your application as soon as possible since selection and interviews are held continually.
   

  

 At Einride, we are innovators, building solutions the world has never seen before – but urgently needs. That’s why we take action, and it’s why we are always eager to be challenged. We know that our best innovations come from having a diverse mix of people, including those of different experiences, career paths, and walks of life. By coming together and sharing our perspectives openly – by disagreeing, discussing, and committing – we deliver greater impact.
   

  

 Please note that as part of our standard recruitment process, we conduct a background control on the final candidate for this role. This may include verification of education, employment history, any relevant professional certifications or other information that may be of our interest.

title: Red Hat OpenShift Architect
description:
Job Title:
 **RedHat OpenShift Engineer** 




 Job Location:
 **Kista, Stockholm, Sweden/ Onsite** 




 Job Type:
 **Contract for 6 months (subject to change)** 




  





  





**Language: English, Swedish is an added advantage** 









  





**Job description for a Red Hat OpenShift Architect** 




 This is a job description for a Red Hat OpenShift Architect with proven experience migrating workloads from VMware (vSphere/onprem VMs) to Red Hat OpenShift (Kubernetes). The role focuses on solution architecture, migration planning and execution, platform automation, and ensuring secure, scalable, and highly available OpenShift-based platforms.
 



**Role Overview** 



* Lead architecture, design, and implementation of enterprise OpenShift platforms.
* Execute migration strategies for moving applications and data from VMware virtual machines and traditional architectures to containerized OpenShift environments.
* Work with cross-functional teams (Dev, Security, Networking, Storage) to deliver migration projects with minimal disruption and measurable business value.



**Key Responsibilities** 



* Experience in end-to-end OpenShift platform architectures (high-availability, multi-AZ, multi-cluster, hybrid cloud).
* Create migration strategies (lift-and-shift, replatform, refactor) for VMware-hosted workloads to OpenShift, including phased migration plans and cutover/rollback procedures.
* Lead proofs-of-concept (PoCs), pilot migrations, and production migrations; validate performance, resilience, and security post-migration.
* Build automation for platform provisioning and day‑2 operations using Infrastructure as Code (Ansible, Terraform, OpenShift Ansible/Installer, Operators).
* Define networking, storage, and security models for OpenShift (CNI selection, persistent storage via CSI, RBAC, network policies, service mesh considerations).
* Integrate CI/CD and GitOps pipelines (e.g., Jenkins/GitLab/ArgoCD) for automated app delivery to OpenShift.
* Establish observability and logging (Prometheus, Grafana, EFK/OLM) and implement backup/restore strategies (Velero or equivalent).
* Collaborate with VMware/vSphere teams to assess virtualization inventories, dependencies, and create migration timelines.
* Produce architecture artifacts: diagrams, runbooks, migration playbooks, capacity and cost estimates, and operational runbooks for SRE teams.
* Mentor engineering teams on containerization best practices, operator patterns, and cloud-native application design.
* Ensure compliance with security, governance, and regulatory requirements during and after migration.



**Required Qualifications & Experience** 



* Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or equivalent experience.
* 8+ years in enterprise infrastructure/cloud roles, with at least 3–5 years specifically designing and operating Red Hat OpenShift or Kubernetes platforms.
* Hands-on experience migrating applications and services from VMware/vSphere (on-prem) to OpenShift/Kubernetes.
* Strong knowledge of VMware stack: vSphere, vCenter, and familiarity with virtualization concepts, VM inventories, and dependency mapping.
* Expertise in OpenShift installation and configuration (IPI/UPI), cluster lifecycle, and operator model.
* Proficiency with Infrastructure-as-Code and automation tools (Terraform, Ansible, OpenShift CLI/oc).
* Solid understanding of networking (CNI plugins, load balancing, DNS), persistent storage and CSI drivers, and storage backends (NFS, Ceph/Rook, SAN).
* Experience with CI/CD and GitOps integration for container deployments.
* Familiar with backup, migration, and application portability tools (e.g., Velero, Red Hat Migrations/operand tools, image registries).
* Strong scripting skills (Bash, Python) and familiarity with container tooling (Docker, Podman).
* Experience defining security posture for Kubernetes/OpenShift (RBAC, OPA/Gatekeeper, secrets management).
* Excellent stakeholder management, communication, and documentation skills.



**Preferred / Nice-to-have** 



* Red Hat certifications: Red Hat Certified Specialist in OpenShift Administration, RHCE, or RHCA.
* Experience with data migration for stateful applications and databases.
* Prior experience running large-scale multi-cluster OpenShift environments and hybrid cloud connectivity (VPN, Direct Connect, ExpressRoute).



**Success Metrics / KPIs** 



* Percentage of workloads successfully migrated within planned windows and with minimal downtime.
* Number of migrations completed per quarter and time-to-migration per application class.
* Post-migration application performance and availability compared to pre-migration baselines.
* Reduction in infrastructure cost or operational overhead after migration.
* Automation coverage for provisioning and day‑2 operations (percentage automated tasks).
* Number of runbooks and documentation items completed and validated by operations teams.



**Deliverables** 



* Migration strategy document and phased migration roadmap.
* Architecture diagrams and sizing/capacity plans.
* Migration playbooks, cutover and rollback procedures.
* Automated deployment scripts and IaC modules.
* Operational runbooks for monitoring, backup, and disaster recovery.
* Post-migration validation and performance reports.

title: Industry Sales Specialist - IoT Connectivity (Western Europe and Middle East)
description:
**About Webbing**
 Webbing delivers carrier-grade, software-driven, AI-powered global cellular connectivity for mission-critical environments.
   

  

 We power real-time video, data, devices, and decisions across 600+ networks in 190+ countries—supporting industries such as broadcast, logistics, industrial IoT, telemetry, monitoring systems, smart infrastructure, and connected operations worldwide.
   

  

 The Opportunity
   

  

 We are building a sales specialist position in the global IoT connectivity market—a multi-billion dollar, high-growth space driven by automation, AI, edge computing, real-time data, and connected infrastructure.
   

  

 From enabling live broadcast operations and industrial telemetry to powering logistics visibility, remote monitoring, and mission-critical field systems—connectivity is the backbone that keeps modern businesses operating in real time.
   

  

 We already serve an all-star global customer base. Now we’re accelerating growth.
   

  

 This is a chance to own and shape the IoT market, driving:
   

  

* New business
* Account expansion
* A partner ecosystem that creates market gravity and scale


 The Role
   

  

 This is a US-based, direct, quota-carrying sales role.
